go:80029.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-I
go:80029.question: The most satisfactory LED driver circuit using a TTL gate is:

go:80032.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-II
go:80032.question:
The total number of Boolean functions which can be realised with four variables is:

  1. $4$
  2. $17$
  3. $256$
  4. $65, 536$

go:80034.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-III
go:80034.question:
The above circuit produces the output sequence:

  1. $1111\quad 1111\quad0000\quad0000$
  2. $1111 \quad 0000\quad1111\quad0000$
  3. $1111 \quad 0001\quad0011\quad0101$
  4. $1010\quad1010\quad1010\quad1010$

go:80193.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-IV
go:80193.question:
The output $F$ of the below multiplexer circuit can be represented by

  1. $AB+B\bar{C}+\bar{C}A+\bar{B}\bar{C}$
  2. $A\oplus B\oplus C$
  3. $A \oplus B$
  4. $\bar{A} \bar{B} C+ \bar{A} B \bar{C}+A \bar{B} \bar{C}$

go:80194.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-V
go:80194.question:
The most relevant addressing mode to write position-independent codes is:

  1. Direct mode
  2. Indirect mode
  3. Relative mode
  4. Indexed mode

go:80199.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-vi
go:80199.question:
A microprogrammed control unit

  1. Is faster than a hard-wired control unit.
  2. Facilitates easy implementation of new instruction.
  3. Is useful when very small programs are to be run.
  4. Usually refers to the control unit of a microprocessor.

go:80201.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-vii
go:80201.question:
The exponent of a floating-point number is represented in excess-$\text{N}$ code so that:

  1. The dynamic range is large.
  2. The precision is high.
  3. The smallest number is represented by all zeros.
  4. Overflow is avoided.

go:80274.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-viii
go:80274.question:
On receiving an interrupt from a I/O device the CPU:

  1. Halts for a predetermined time.
  2. Hands over control of address bus and data bus to the interrupting device.
  3. Branches off to the interrupt service routine immediately.
  4. Branches off to the interrupt service routine after completion of the current instruction.

go:80282.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xi
go:80282.question:
In a compiler the module that checks every character of the source text is called:

  1. The code generator.
  2. The code optimiser.
  3. The lexical analyser.
  4. The syntax analyser.

go:80291.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xii
go:80291.question:
A context-free grammar is ambiguous if:

  1. The grammar contains useless non-terminals.
  2. It produces more than one parse tree for some sentence.
  3. Some production has two non terminals side by side on the right-hand side.
  4. None of the above.

go:80293.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xiii
go:80293.question:
FORTRAN is a:

  1. Regular language.
  2. Context-free language.
  3. Context-senstive language.
  4. None of the above.

go:80295.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xiv
go:80295.question:
An operator precedence parser is a

  1. Bottom-up parser.
  2. Top-down parser.
  3. Back tracking parser.
  4. None of the above.

go:80373.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xix
go:80373.question:
Study the following program written in a block-structured language:

Var x, y:interger; 
procedure P(n:interger);
begin
     x:=(n+2)/(n-3);
end;

procedure Q 
Var x, y:interger;
begin   
    x:=3;
    y:=4; 
    P(y);
    Write(x)                                __(1)
end;
 
begin
    x:=7;
    y:=8;
    Q; 
Write(x);                                   __(2) 
end.

What will be printed by the write statements marked $(1)$ and $(2)$ in the program if the variables are statically scoped?

  1. $3, 6$
  2. $6, 7$
  3. $3, 7$
  4. None of the above.

go:80298.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xv
go:80298.question:
In a circular linked list organization, insertion of a record involves modification of

  1. One pointer.
  2. Two pointers.
  3. Multiple pointers.
  4. No pointer.

go:80362.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xvi
go:80362.question:
A critical region is

  1. One which is enclosed by a pair of $P$ and $V$ operations on semaphores.
  2. A program segment that has not been proved bug-free.
  3. A program segment that often causes unexpected system crashes.
  4. A program segment where shared resources are accessed.

go:80364.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xvii
go:80364.question:
Using longer identifiers in a program will necessarily lead to:

  1. Somewhat slower compilation
  2. A program that is easier to understand
  3. An incorrect program
  4. None of the above

go:80366.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xviii
go:80366.question:
Let $P$ be a quicksort program to sort numbers in ascending order. Let $t_{1}$ and $t_{2}$ be the time taken by the program for the inputs $\left[1 \ 2 \ 3 \ 4\right]$ and $\left[5 \ 4 \ 3 \ 2 \ 1\right]$, respectively. Which of the following holds?

  1. $t_{1} = t_{2}$
  2. $t_{1} > t_{2}$
  3. $t_{1} < t_{2}$
  4. $t_{1}=t_{2}+5 \log 5$

go:80374.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xx
go:80374.question:
For the program given below what will be printed by the write statements marked $(1)$ and $(2)$ in the program if the variables are dynamically scoped?

Var x, y:interger; 
procedure P(n:interger);
begin
     x := (n+2)/(n-3);
end;

procedure Q 
Var x, y:interger;
begin   
    x:=3;
    y:=4; 
    P(y);
    Write(x);                                __(1)
end;
 
begin
    x:=7;
    y:=8;
    Q; 
    Write(x);                                   __(2)
end.
  1. $3, 6$
  2. $6, 7$
  3. $3, 7$
  4. None of the above

go:80377.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xxi
go:80377.question:
If $a, b,$ and $c$ are constants, which of the following is a linear inequality?

  1. $ax+bcy=0$
  2. $ax^{2}+cy^{2}=21$
  3. $abx+a^{2}y \geq 15$
  4. $xy+ax \geq 20$

go:80379.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xxii
go:80379.question:
The equation $7x^{7}+14x^{6}+12x^{5}+3x^{4}+12x^{3}+10x^{2}+5x+7=0$ has

  1. All complex roots
  2. At least one real root
  3. Four pairs of imaginary roots
  4. None of the above

go:80380.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xxiii
go:80380.question:
A square matrix is singular whenever 

  1. The rows are linearly independent
  2. The columns are linearly independent
  3. The row are linearly dependent
  4. None of the above

go:80559.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xxiv
go:80559.question:
The simplex method is so named because 

  1. It is simple.
  2. It is based on the theory of algebraic complexes.
  3. The simple pendulum works on this method.
  4. No one thought of a better name.

go:80562.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xxv
go:80562.question:
Which of the following statements is true in respect of the convergence of the Newton-Rephson procedure?

  1. It converges always under all circumstances.
  2. It does not converge to a tool where the second differential coefficient changes sign.
  3. It does not converge to a root where the second differential coefficient vanishes.
  4. None of the above.

go:80571.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 1-xxvi
go:80571.question:
If $f(x_{i}).f(x_{i+1})< 0$ then

  1. There must be a root of $f(x)$ between $x_i$ and $x_{i+1}$
  2. There need not be a root of $f(x)$ between $x_{i}$ and $x_{i+1}$
  3. There fourth derivative of $f(x)$ with respect to $x$ vanishes at $x_{i}$
  4. The fourth derivative of $f(x)$ with respect to $x$ vanishes at $x_{i+1}$

go:80572.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2a
go:80572.question:
State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE

In a microprocessor-based system, if a bus (DMA) request and an interrupt request arrive sumultaneously, the microprocessor attends first to the bus request.

go:80576.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2b
go:80576.question:
State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE:

Data transfer between a microprocessor and an I/O device is usually faster in memory-mapped-I/O scheme than in I/O-mapped -I/O scheme.

go:80579.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2c
go:80579.question:
State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE:

It is possible to construct a binary tree uniquely whose pre-order and post-order traversals are given?

go:80583.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2d
go:80583.question:
State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE:

The union of two equivalence relations is also an equivalence relation.

go:80588.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2g
go:80588.question:
State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE:

If the number of leaves in a tree is not a power of $2,$ then the tree is not a binary tree.

go:80589.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2h
go:80589.question:
State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE:

Regularity is preserved under the operation of string reversal.

go:80590.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2i
go:80590.question:
State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE:

All subsets of regular sets are regular.

go:80594.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2j
go:80594.question:
State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE:

A minimal DFA that is equivalent to an NDFA with $n$ nodes has always $2^{n}$ states.

go:80599.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2k
go:80599.question:
State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE:

The intersection of two CFL's is also a CFL.

go:80603.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2l
go:80603.question:
State whether the following statement are TRUE or FALSE.

$A$ is recursive if both $A$ and its complement are accepted by Turing machines.

go:80606.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2m
go:80606.question:
State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE:

The problem as to whether a Turing machine $M$ accepts input $w$ is undecidable.

go:80609.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 2n
go:80609.question:
State whether the following statements are TRUE or FALSE:

A relation $r$ with schema $(X, Y)$ satisfies the function dependency $X \rightarrow Y$, The tuples $\langle 1, 2\rangle$ and $\langle 2, 2 \rangle$ can both be in $r$ simultaneously.

go:82548.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 11a
go:82548.question: Given $f(300)=2,4771; f(304) = 2.4829; f(305) = 2.4843$ and $f(307) = 2.4871$ find $f(301)$ using Lagrange's interpolation formula.

go:82550.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 11b
go:82550.question: Use Simpson's rule with $h=0.25$ to evaluate $ V= \int_{0}^{1} \frac{1}{1+x} dx$ correct to three decimal places.

go:82556.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 12-a
go:82556.question:
The Boolean expression $A \oplus B \oplus A$ is equivalent to

  1. $AB + \overline {A}\;\;\overline B$
  2. $\overline{A}\;B+A\;\overline{B}$
  3. $B$
  4. $\overline{A}$

go:82607.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 13-a
go:82607.question:
The below figure shows four $\text{D}$-type flip-flops connected as a shift register using a $\text{XOR}$ gate. The initial state and three subsequent states for three clock pulses are also given.

$$\begin{array}{l|c|c|c|c} \hline \text{State} & Q_{A} & Q_{B} & Q_{C} & Q_{D} \\\hline \text{Initial} & 1 & 1 & 1 & 1 \\ \text{After the first clock} & 0 & 1 & 1 & 1 \\ \text{After the second clock} & 0 & 0 & 1 & 1 \\ \text{After the third clock} & 0 & 0 & 0 & 1 \\\hline  \end{array}$$The state $Q_{A} Q_{B} Q_{C} Q_{D}$ after the fourth clock pulse is

  1. $0000$
  2. $1111$
  3. $1001$
  4. $1000$

go:82698.title: GATE CSE 1987 | Question: 16-a
go:82698.question:
A Boolean function $f$ is to be realized only by $\text{NOR}$ gates. Its $K$-map is given below:

The realization is

  1.  

  2.  

  3.  

  4.
